author: @gameyord7182
text: few adjustments i would make, put the magnet in the arm inside the coil, and use a wide and flar paper cone, plus a relief around it to let it move in and out, but besides that it is perfect! cheers M8!

author: @jozefbubez6116
text: Most interesting! Speakers of this kind seem to emit a very characteristic sound; not exactly hi-fi but good electo-acoustic efficiency compared to the more usual moving-coil.

author: @kattlesheets9991
text: Okay, my interested. Would you by chance be willing to part with a working sketch of your armature. I would like to expound upon the mechanics and possible efficiencies to be made.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: kattle sheets it's all on the vid mate - there's nothing to add

author: @JohnSmith-eo5sp
text:
I do not know about your country but the balanced armature speaker was largely abandoned in America by the 1940s in favor of dynamic speakers. I believe the sound-powered telephones use powerful rare earth magnets to bias them.
The balanced armature speaker was a key component of the German 'People's Radio's' which was commisioned by the Nazis in1933.
